comment: Flow_FinalizeEvaBox (§5.10 / phase-2 M2.C+M2.D; Phase-7 BOX-AUGMENT DELTA, ADR-0012 / build-plan 04 / box-flow-patterns Fragment 3). EVA + Box finalize in unison inside one Scope. PHASE-7 CHANGE: the UPPERCASE Case/PO folder now PRE-EXISTS — it is minted at parse-confirm by box-folder-create (custom connector shared_box_rest CreateFolder). Finalize therefore AUGMENTS the folder (uploads photo bytes + the .eva.json into it); it does NOT create it. The dead hard-coded BoxArchiveRootId flow parameter is REMOVED and replaced by a READ of env-var cr1bd_BOX_FOLDER_ROOT_ID (Get_box_root_definition / Set_box_root_id) for cross-section consistency with box-folder-create (dataverse owns the name; build-plan 04 §step-2 hand-off). The first-party shared_box CreateFile byte path is UNCHANGED and folderPath-based (it lands in / auto-finds the pre-existing UPPERCASE folder), so the migration touches no byte op. Box folder = UPPERCASE Case/PO; EVA uses lowercase - same identity, two casings. Photo order = 2 previews first (overview w/ full registration + damage closeup) then ALL photos incl. those two again (Evidence sequenceIndex asc). The Sentry REST submit is a TWO-REQUEST photo flow inside the evasentry Function (confirmed against Sentry API v1.2 PDF pp.13,21-23): the 2 preview photos ride as EVA Files {Name,Extension,Data} on POST /Instruction/Inspection (creates the claim, returns Id), then the full ordered set rides on POST /Note/SubmitNote matched by VehReg (+ClmNo) - so the flow passes body/vrm (cr1bd_vrm) and body/clmNo (cr1bd_caseref). EVA_API_ENABLED gates the TRANSPORT (Sentry REST via cr1bd_evasentry when true; JSON drag-drop export staged to Box when false - M1 default + PERMANENT fallback) not the finalization. The Sentry REST token lifecycle lives INSIDE the evasentry Function (Power Platform custom connectors cannot do the OAuth2 client-credentials grant - Microsoft Learn), keyed by function-key; the flow never reads EVA_CLIENT_SECRET. Idempotent by cr1bd_finalizedpayloadhash (stamped LAST; the Function also dedups by payload hash in-process). [BUILD]. BOX BYTE CONTRACT (verified Microsoft Learn connectors/box + connectors/azureblobconnector 2026-06-20): the first-party Box connector is class STANDARD and has NO CreateFolder op — BYTES upload via CreateFile (params folderPath/name/binary body) into the pre-existing folder; the custom shared_box_rest connector ops NEVER appear in this byte path (folder creation moved to box-folder-create). S2 FIX: each photo is read from Azure Blob via Get blob content using path (V2) (cr1bd_evidenceblob, dataset=AccountNameFromSettings) and its REAL bytes go to Box - previously the cr1bd_storagepath PATH STRING was uploaded as the body (silent corruption: a tiny text file, not the image). The .eva.json drag-drop body is a JSON string = already content, so it needs no blob read. ACTIVATION [RESERVED-FOR-USER]: bind cr1bd_box (live Box, interactive OAuth - the connector supports no JWT/CCG) + cr1bd_evidenceblob (Azure Blob, account cespkevidstdev01, access-key, dataset=AccountNameFromSettings) + cr1bd_evasentry, set cr1bd_BOX_FOLDER_ROOT_ID (or root the Box connection at the archive parent so folderPath is just /<CASEPO>/), inject EVA test Client_Id/Secret into Key Vault, set EVA_REQUEST_FROM (EVA-supplied contact code) app setting, confirm the pre-existing UPPERCASE folder is found + the UPPERCASE casing (B5/H6), flip EVA_API_ENABLED=true in TEST only, run a parity test (drag-drop==API) before any prod cutover. Box can go live on the drag-drop transport (EVA_API_ENABLED=false) BEFORE the Sentry REST path exists. Claude never injects secrets, binds a live account, or flips the prod gate.

When_submit_requested.comment: PHASE-7 (ADR-0012 / 00-BUILD-PLAN reconciliation 'Code App -> flow invocation', which WINS over plan-04's stale Request-trigger note): finalize is invoked by a DATAVERSE SUBMIT-SIGNAL, not an HTTP/SAS POST (the Code App runs under CSP connect-src 'none' and never POSTs to a flow URL). The Code App's makeDataverseFinalizeTransport PATCHes cr1bd_submitrequested=true (+ cr1bd_submitpayloadhash + cr1bd_evapayload12); this 'When a row is added, modified or deleted' trigger (SdkMessage Update=3, scope Organization=4) fires only when cr1bd_submitrequested is part of the update (filteringattributes) AND evaluates true (filterexpression), then the flow reads caseId/payloadHash/evaPayload12 from the triggering ROW (a row trigger exposes only the row, not an HTTP body). The flag is RESET to false LAST (Stamp_finalized_hash) so it is the trigger boundary; re-running requires a fresh app request, and the cr1bd_finalizedpayloadhash latch still blocks a duplicate-payload re-submit. [RESERVED-FOR-USER] at activation: this Dataverse trigger registers a CallbackRegistration when the flow is turned on (no Office-365 webhook to re-arm — unlike intake; the flow-webhook-trigger-provisioning gotcha does not apply here, but the flow is state=off until the operator activates it).

Init_caseId.comment: PHASE-7: caseId is the PRIMARY KEY of the triggering Case row (the row that had cr1bd_submitrequested set true), not an HTTP body field.

Init_payloadHash.comment: PHASE-7: the requested byte-identical hash the Code App wrote with the signal (cr1bd_submitpayloadhash), kept DISTINCT from the cr1bd_finalizedpayloadhash latch so the request does not pre-empt the stamped-LAST resume-safety.

Init_evaPayload12.comment: PHASE-7: the schema-valid 12-field EVA JSON in contract order, read VERBATIM from the staged cr1bd_evapayload12 column the Code App PATCHed (built by the SAME serializer the Code App uses - eva-export.ts / eva-payload.schema.json - so it stays byte-identical for both the drag-drop body and the API body; the flow does NOT re-serialize the 12 cr1bd_eva* columns, which would risk format drift). vrm/reference are NOT in the 12.

Init_evaImages.comment: M2.C photo streaming: the ordered base-64 photo set for the EVA REST submit (body/images). Initialised at root scope (a variable cannot be declared inside the Scope/Foreach) then appended to once per photo INSIDE Upload_photos_in_eva_order, reusing the SAME blob bytes the loop already reads for the Box upload (no second blob read). Stays empty on the drag-drop path (gate off), so it is harmless when unused.

Set_gate_EVA_API_ENABLED.comment: M1 default false -> JSON drag-drop export path (permanent fallback).

Get_box_root_definition.comment: PHASE-7 MIGRATION: READ the archive root id from env-var cr1bd_BOX_FOLDER_ROOT_ID (replaces the removed hard-coded BoxArchiveRootId flow parameter; dataverse owns the name). The first-party CreateFile byte path is folderPath-based and lands in the pre-existing UPPERCASE folder, so this value is read for cross-section consistency/observability (and a future folder-id-based op) — it is NEVER a hardcoded live Box id.

Set_box_root_id.comment: The archive root Box folder id (currentValue -> defaultValue -> ''). Read from Dataverse, never a literal.

Guard_already_finalized.comment: Idempotency latch: re-running finalize for an already-submitted payload hash must not double-submit or re-create the folder. TRUE branch (hash is NEW) runs the finalize Scope. ELSE branch (hash ALREADY finalized = a duplicate-payload submit): NO finalize work runs (no EVA submit, no Box augment) — it ONLY consumes the stuck submit-signal by resetting cr1bd_submitrequested=false (otherwise the Code App's signal is never cleared and the case stays apparently 'submit pending' forever) and writes a duplicate-submit-ignored audit.

Order_evidence.comment: Image Evidence accepted for EVA, ascending sequenceIndex (2 previews seeded at index 0,1 then full sequence incl. those two again).

parameters.$filter: _cr1bd_caseid_value eq @{variables('caseId')} and cr1bd_kind eq 100000000 and cr1bd_acceptedforeva eq true and cr1bd_excluded eq false

Upload_photos_in_eva_order.comment: repetitions=1 preserves EVA upload order: 2 previews first, then full sequence incl. those two. The UPPERCASE Case/PO folder is created IMPLICITLY by writing into CreateFile's folderPath (the first-party Box connector has NO CreateFolder op — confirmed on Microsoft Learn connectors/box 2026-06-20; the deleted Create_box_folder_UPPERCASE invented one). Each photo is a Get_blob_content_v2 -> Copy_evidence_to_box pair (S2 content-bind fix).

Get_blob_content_v2.comment: S2 FIX: read the REAL photo bytes from the evidence Azure Blob before the Box upload. cr1bd_storagepath is an Azure Blob PATH STRING (e.g. /evidence/intake/<messageId>/IMG.jpg), NOT bytes — uploading it as the Box body wrote a tiny text file, not the image (silent corruption). Get blob content using path (V2) (operationId GetFileContentByPath_V2; the non-V2 GetFileContentByPath is DEPRECATED) returns binary; dataset=AccountNameFromSettings matches the classify-persist storage convention (account cespkevidstdev01, access-key auth). Implicit chunking 50MB no-chunk / up to 1024MB chunked, so the read side comfortably exceeds the 75MB Box write ceiling. [RESERVED-FOR-USER]: bind cr1bd_evidenceblob (Azure Blob, access key) at activation so this read works.

Copy_evidence_to_box.comment: Box Create file (operationId CreateFile; verified params folderPath/name/body on Microsoft Learn connectors/box 2026-06-20 — there is NO folderId param on CreateFile, folderId was a TRIGGER param). folderPath = '/' + UPPERCASE Case/PO (e.g. EVA test26001 -> Box /TEST26001/) auto-creates the folder (SharePoint-style; CONFIRM on first live run — open question 1). body = the real blob bytes from Get_blob_content_v2 (S2 fix). Box known-issue #6: a same-name re-upload is an UPDATE not a 409, so the loop is resume-safe by filename. [RESERVED-FOR-USER]: bind cr1bd_box to the live Box account + set BoxArchiveRootId at activation; confirm Box honours the UPPERCASE casing (B5).

Append_eva_image.comment: M2.C: collect ONE PhotoEntry per photo into evaImages, in the SAME EVA-export order the loop iterates (Order_evidence is sequenceIndex asc; concurrency repetitions=1 keeps the append order = upload order). Shape mirrors the evasentry connector PhotoEntry (functions/evasentry/openapi + payload.py build_files/split_preview_and_rest): sequenceIndex (cr1bd_sequenceindex), content = base64 of the SAME bytes just read by Get_blob_content_v2 for the Box upload (no second blob read — proves they are reachable), role = cr1bd_imagerole int mapped to the enum string (overview=100000000 / damage_closeup=100000001 / additional=100000002 / else unknown), filename (cr1bd_filename -> EVA File Name+Extension), registrationVisible (cr1bd_registrationvisible, advisory for the overview check). The Function re-sorts by sequenceIndex and splits previews/rest server-side, so this is order-robust either way. NOTE on size: each photo is base64 here (~+33% over the raw bytes); the whole evaImages array rides in ONE connector request body to the evasentry Function. The Box write ceiling (75MB per file) is per-file, but the EVA REST body is the SUM of all photos base64 — for a large case this can be heavy; the Function performs the two-request split server-side but the inbound connector body is still one payload. If a case's combined base64 approaches the Logic Apps / connector message-size limit, the drag-drop transport (EVA_API_ENABLED=false, photos already archived to Box) remains the fallback. Flagged for the EVA TEST cutover.

EVA_instruction_inspection.comment: Sentry REST path - the evasentry Function mints/caches the 5-min /Connect/token JWT and attaches the bearer SERVER-SIDE (Key Vault refs); this connector is function-keyed only. The Function maps evaPayload12 (the schema-valid 12-field core, byte-identical to the drag-drop body) to the PascalCase Instruction/Inspection model and performs the TWO-REQUEST photo submission server-side (2 previews on /Instruction/Inspection, then the full set on /Note/SubmitNote). payloadHash echoes the idempotency latch; casePo (lowercase) -> ExternalRef; vrm (cr1bd_vrm) -> VehReg and clmNo (cr1bd_caseref) -> ClmNo are the claim keys the Note request uses to attach the remaining photos. Runs only when EVA_API_ENABLED=true. M2.C PHOTO STREAMING (now wired): body/images carries the ordered base-64 photo set (evaImages, built in Upload_photos_in_eva_order from the SAME bytes already read for the Box upload) as [{sequenceIndex, content(base64), role, filename, registrationVisible}] matching the connector PhotoEntry shape - the Function re-sorts by sequenceIndex and splits previews/rest, so a live REST submit now attaches the photos (previously only the 12-field core went, attaching ZERO photos). [RESERVED-FOR-USER] to enable/cutover; the drag-drop transport stays the fallback if a case's combined base64 body is too large for the connector.

Stage_drag_drop_json.comment: M1 default: emit the schema-valid 12-field JSON for staff drag-drop into EVA. Same serializer as the Code App - byte-identical. Box Create file with the path-based contract (folderPath, not folderId): folderPath = '/' + UPPERCASE Case/PO so the .eva.json lands in the SAME single per-case folder as the photos; name = lowercase Case/PO + '.eva.json'. A JSON string is already text content, so NO Get_blob_content is needed here (only the photo bytes needed the S2 fix).

Stamp_finalized_hash.comment: Stamp LAST = idempotency latch. status box_synced=100000009 (terminal). cr1bd_boxsyncedat is RESTAMPED here at actual Box-sync time (PHASE-7): box-blob-purge measures its grace window off cr1bd_boxsyncedat, so without restamping at finalize the grace would be measured from box-folder-create's parse-confirm folder-create time — a long-open case (folder minted weeks before finalize) could become purge-eligible immediately on reaching box_synced. Restamping here ties the purge grace to when the bytes actually landed in Box. cr1bd_submitrequested is RESET to false here so the Dataverse submit-signal is consumed - the flag is the trigger boundary, so re-running requires a fresh Code App request; the latch (cr1bd_finalizedpayloadhash vs cr1bd_submitpayloadhash) additionally blocks a duplicate-payload re-submit. A partial failure leaves the latch unstamped AND the flag still true so a re-run resumes cleanly. NOTE: this UpdateRecord modifies cr1bd_submitrequested, which is a filteringattribute of this flow's own trigger; the filterexpression (cr1bd_submitrequested eq true) means setting it FALSE does not satisfy the trigger, so no self-retrigger loop occurs.

Reset_submit_signal_duplicate.comment: DUPLICATE-PAYLOAD path (cr1bd_finalizedpayloadhash already equals the requested payloadHash): the finalize Scope is SKIPPED (no re-submit, no Box re-augment, no status restamp). But the Code App's submit-signal cr1bd_submitrequested is still TRUE from the request that retriggered this flow — if we did nothing it would stay stuck TRUE forever and the case would look 'submit pending'. Reset it to false here to consume the signal WITHOUT doing any finalize work. (Setting it FALSE does not satisfy the trigger's filterexpression cr1bd_submitrequested eq true, so no self-retrigger.)

parameters.item/cr1bd_after: @concat('duplicate submit ignored — payload hash ', variables('payloadHash'), ' already finalized; submit-signal reset, NO re-submit / NO Box re-augment / NO status change')

Audit_duplicate_submit_ignored.comment: Audit the ignored duplicate submit. Reuses action eva_submitted=100000015 (the closest existing value) with explicit 'duplicate submit ignored' semantics in name/after — the SAME no-new-value reuse precedent as the rest of this file (e.g. box_synced reusing 100000016); the dataverse choiceset owns the vocabulary. Linked to the case. NO finalize work ran on this branch.
